evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints (oauth2/token, users/@me, users/@me/guilds, applications/{app_id}/guilds/{guild_id}/commands) and v10 API base, Bot vs Bearer token usage, and scopes (identify, guilds) are internally consistent and match well-known Discord API surface. Long-tail handling: consistent + version v10 claimed = 4.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse cleanly; imports defined; FastAPI + React boilerplate works. Minor: token exchange should include Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded header (httpx defaults to form encoding when data= dict, so likely fine). No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo).
evidence.completeness: All 6 pieces present: API key acquisition with URL+menu paths, install bash blocks, .env templates (backend+frontend) with exact names matching code, backend code, frontend code, runnable test commands. Env var names match between .env and code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only Discord Developer Portal link (https://discord.com/developers/applications) cited; no link to discord.com/developers/docs near OAuth2 or application commands code. One official-ish URL but not docs proper.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable; applied LONG-TAIL HANDLING. Internal consistency strong; v10 explicit. Citation weak — only portal link, no docs reference URLs placed near code.
